original_text:
### Bolding Rules **[GENERAL — calibrated to the guide's callout/chart patterns]**
- Bold is for **hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration**. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.
- Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).
- Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, the generic name.
- Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.
- No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

relations.note: The emphasis-device limit constrains how approved bold emphasis may be combined with ALL CAPS and color.

quotes: ### Bolding Rules **[GENERAL — calibrated to the guide's callout/chart patterns]**
Bold is for **hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration**. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.
Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).
Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, the generic name.

rules.intent: Keep bolding selective so hierarchy and clinically meaningful takeaways remain scannable.
rules.rule_text: Use bold for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration. In each email section, bold only heading(s), no more than one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical-callout figure. Always bold callout text as specified for its callout treatment, statistical-callout numbers, and the brand name when it is the focal point of a benefit-claim sentence, but only on its first instance in that section. Never bold entire paragraphs, footnotes or references, more than approximately 15% of running body text in a section, prepositional filler, or the generic name.

quotes: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

rules.intent: Prevent compounded emphasis from reducing clarity and visual hierarchy.
rules.rule_text: Do not combine bold, ALL CAPS, and color emphasis on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Use at most two emphasis devices per element.

quotes: No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

rules.intent: Preserve familiar hyperlink affordance and keep patient-copy emphasis clear.
rules.rule_text: Underline only real hyperlinks. In patient copy, do not use italics for emphasis; reserve italics for study names, notes such as "Note:", and citations.
